lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: Fellows in arms, and my most loving friends,

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: Bruised underneath the yoke of tyranny,

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: Thus far into the bowels of the land

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: Have we march’d on without impediment;

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: And here receive we from our father Stanley

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: Lines of fair comfort and encouragement.

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: The wretched, bloody, and usurping boar,

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: That spoil’d your summer fields and fruitful vines,

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: Swills your warm blood like wash, and makes his trough

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: In your embowell’d bosoms, this foul swine

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: Lies now even in the centre of this isle,

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: Near to the town of Leicester, as we learn:

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: From Tamworth thither is but one day’s march.

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: In God’s name, cheerly on, courageous friends,

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: To reap the harvest of perpetual peace

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: By this one bloody trial of sharp war.

lines.speaker: OXFORD
lines.text: Every man’s conscience is a thousand swords,

lines.speaker: OXFORD
lines.text: To fight against that bloody homicide.

lines.speaker: HERBERT
lines.text: I doubt not but his friends will fly to us.

lines.speaker: BLUNT
lines.text: He hath no friends but who are friends for fear,

lines.speaker: BLUNT
lines.text: Which in his greatest need will shrink from him.

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: All for our vantage. Then, in God’s name, march:

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: True hope is swift, and flies with swallow’s wings;

lines.speaker: RICHMOND
lines.text: Kings it makes gods, and meaner creatures kings.

stage_directions.text: Enter Richmond, Oxford, Blunt, Herbert, and others, with drum and colours.

glosses.definition: oppressive rule — Richmond's opening framing of Richard
Richard as heraldic boar — 'usurping' frames him as illegitimate king

glosses.phrase: Swills your warm blood like wash
glosses.definition: drinks your blood like pig-swill ('wash' = kitchen slops fed to pigs)
Leicester — Richard's base before Bosworth; historically where his remains were rediscovered in 2012
Richmond's staging point, Staffordshire — one day's march from Bosworth

glosses.phrase: one bloody trial of sharp war
Every man’s conscience is a thousand swords
glosses.definition: Oxford's inversion of Richard's 5.3.190 'thousand several tongues' — good conscience arms the army

glosses.phrase: Kings it makes gods, and meaner creatures kings
glosses.definition: hope makes kings divine and common men royal

annotations.title: Richmond's first speech: the boar, the hog, the swine
annotations.body: Richmond's rallying speech uses three different pig-variants for Richard — boar, foul swine, hog — within twenty lines. The escalation is deliberate. The boar is Richard's heraldic badge (white boar of Gloucester); the swine and hog degrade the emblem to the barnyard. "Swills your warm blood like wash" converts Richard into a pig drinking kitchen-slops; the "trough" is made in "your embowell'd bosoms." The imagery cluster answers Margaret's 1.3 "elvish-mark'd, abortive, rooting hog" — Richmond arrives speaking her vocabulary, a detail that partially legitimizes his violence against Richard as the language of the women Richard has wronged. What the speech cannot quite do is give Richmond interiority. He is the play's necessary figure rather than its psychological counterpart, and the speech's calculated hatred lets the audience feel that.

trials.prompt: Richmond's rallying speech uses three pig-variants for Richard (boar, swine, hog). What does this imagery cluster do?
options: Introduces comic deflation before a serious battle
Answers Margaret's 1.3 vocabulary — Richmond arrives speaking the language of the women Richard has wronged, partially legitimizing his cause
Is a classical pastoral reference drawn from Virgil
Is a heraldic argument about the boar's lower standing

trials.prompt: What does Richmond's calculated hatred of Richard reveal about his function in the play's design?
options: That he is the play's psychological centre
That he is the play's necessary figure rather than its psychological counterpart — Shakespeare keeps him schematic so the Tudor dynastic arrival can take on providential shape
That he is a secondary character inserted for historical completeness
That he is the play's secret villain
